steps.observed: Spending Analytics page loads with pie chart showing spending by category
Four period buttons: This Month, Last 3 Months, Last 6 Months, Last 12 Months

steps.expected: Categories shown with amounts and percentages
steps.observed: 9 categories displayed: Rent/Home 62.9% $2,000.00, Groceries 14.1% $448.78, Eating Out 5.9%, etc.

steps.expected: Bar chart showing monthly spending trends
steps.observed: Monthly Spending Trends section with bar chart showing spending from Oct 25 to Mar 26
Bars visible for Jan 26, Feb 26, and Mar 26 with Mar 26 being the tallest (~$3,200)

steps.action: Click Groceries category button in analytics view
steps.observed: Groceries Transactions section appeared with 6 transactions listed
Transactions shown: TEST-AICAT Gas Station Shell $30, TEST-BUD Costco $60, TEST-TXN Split $80, Trader Joe's $67.89, Whole Foods $125.47, Whole Foods $85.42. Total: $448.78
Close drill-down button and X button both present
Manifest returned with name='Personal Finance Tracker', short_name='Finances', display='standalone', theme_color='#3b82f6', background_color='#f8fafc'
Two icons: 192x192 and 512x512, both PNG, both accessible (HTTP 200)
Both /icon-192x192.png and /icon-512x512.png return HTTP 200
Valid manifest with name, icons, display=standalone
Service worker registered with scope http://localhost:3000/, active=true
App served at http://localhost:3000
Dashboard loaded with spending, net worth, budget status, transactions

steps.expected: Offline indicator appears, cached pages still viewable
steps.observed: Yellow banner appeared: 'You are offline. Previously loaded pages are still available.' Dashboard data still visible from cache.
Bottom tab bar with Dashboard, Transactions, Budgets, Accounts, More buttons
Bottom tabs: 75x56px, month nav buttons: 44x44px. All meet minimum.

steps.action: Measure touch targets on transactions page
steps.observed: No elements failed the 44x44px check (empty array from JS measurement of all visible buttons, links, inputs)
Transaction created successfully, no category badge shown

steps.expected: AI categorization runs and assigns category
steps.observed: Toast message: 'Categorized 1 transactions (0 by rules, 1 by AI)'. Transaction 'TEST-APWA: Starbucks Coffee' now shows 'Eating Out' category badge.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. Analytics page has working pie chart with time period selectors, bar chart for monthly trends, and category drill-down. PWA has valid manifest, active service worker, offline caching with indicator, and properly sized touch targets. AI auto-categorization successfully categorized an uncategorized Starbucks Coffee transaction as 'Eating Out'.
